perturbation.original_text: Consultant agrees to act as a consultant to Company with respect to such matters and projects as are mutually agreed from time to time by and between Consultant and Company, and perform the services described on Exhibit A hereto (collectively, "Services"). Company agrees to pay Consultant the compensation set forth on Exhibit A hereto for the performance of the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant agrees to act as a consultant to Company with respect to such matters and projects as are mutually agreed from time to time by and between Consultant and Company, and perform the tasks described on Exhibit A hereto (collectively, "Services"). Company agrees to pay Consultant the compensation set forth on Exhibit A hereto for the performance of the Services.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Services' is initially defined as tasks described in exhibit A. However, by interchanging the word 'Services' with the word 'tasks', it creates an in-text contradiction on the real nature of what is to be performed by the consultant. This change introduces uncertainty in what constitutes the consultant's obligations under the agreement, specifically relating to whether the company is only requesting tasks to be performed and not whole services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Services and Compensation. Consultant agrees to act as a consultant to Company with respect to such matters and projects as are mutually agreed from time to time by and between Consultant and Company, and perform the tasks described on Exhibit A hereto (collectively, "Services"). Company agrees to pay Consultant the compensation set forth on Exhibit A hereto for the performance of the Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Services' as the matters and projects agreed upon and the services described in Exhibit A. The changed text replaces 'services' with 'tasks' in the phrase 'tasks described on Exhibit A hereto (collectively, "Services")'. While both texts still define Services as related to Exhibit A, the change from 'services described' to 'tasks described' introduces an ambiguity about the scope of the consultant's responsibilities, implying that the consultant is only required to perform tasks and not provide full services as might be expected. This contradicts the intention of defining 'Services', creating an inconsistency in the contract regarding the consultant's obligations. The contradicted text contains the same flawed definition ('tasks described') furthering the in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information also includes all Inventions (as defined below) and any other information or materials generated in connection with the Services. Consultant shall not, during or subsequent to the term of this Agreement, use any Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever other than the performance of the Services on behalf of Company, or disclose Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information also includes all Inventions (as defined below) and any other information or materials generated in connection with the Consulting Work. Consultant shall not, during or subsequent to the term of this Agreement, use any Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever other than the performance of the Services on behalf of Company, or disclose Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'Consulting Work' is introduced here without a formal definition. Throughout the document, the defined term 'Services' is used to refer to the consultant's responsibilities. By replacing 'Services' with 'Consulting Work', it becomes unclear if 'Consulting Work' is distinct from 'Services', creating ambiguity in the consultant's obligations and scope of work. This contradicts the existing definition of 'Services'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality. "Confidential Information" means any proprietary information technical data, trade secrets or know-how, including, but not limited to, research and product plans, products, services, markets, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, marketing, finances or other business information disclosed to Consultant by Company either directly or indirectly in writing, orally or otherwise. Confidential Information also includes all Inventions (as defined below) and any other information or materials generated in connection with the Consulting Work. Consultant shall not, during or subsequent to the term of this Agreement, use any Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever other than the performance of the Services on behalf of Company, or disclose Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces the term 'Consulting Work' while the agreement relies on the defined term 'Services.' This creates ambiguity because 'Consulting Work' is not defined, leading to uncertainty about whether it's distinct from 'Services.' The contradictory effect is highlighted in the Contradicted Text where the Consultant's obligations are specified in relation to 'Services.' By using 'Consulting Work' in the Confidentiality clause, it creates confusion about which activities and information are protected under the confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant agrees that if, in the course of performing the Services, Consultant incorporates into any Invention developed hereunder any invention, improvement, development concept, discovery or other proprietary subject matter owned by Consultant or in which Consultant has an interest ("Item"), Consultant will inform Company in writing thereof, and Company is hereby granted and shall have a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license to make, have made, modify, reproduce, display, use and sell such Item as part of or in connection with the exploitation of such Invention.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant agrees that if, in the course of carrying out the Consulting, Consultant incorporates into any Invention developed hereunder any invention, improvement, development concept, discovery or other proprietary subject matter owned by Consultant or in which Consultant has an interest ("Item"), Consultant will inform Company in writing thereof, and Company is hereby granted and shall have a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license to make, have made, modify, reproduce, display, use and sell such Item as part of or in connection with the exploitation of such Invention.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'performing the Services' with 'carrying out the Consulting' introduces a discrepancy. The defined term is 'Services,' which refers to the work outlined in Exhibit A. The undefined term 'Consulting' implies that the consultant's actions are viewed separately, thereby causing a misalignment on which terms refer to the same task/service in question.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership. Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns to Company all right, title and interest in and to any information (including, without limitation, business plans and/or business information), technology, know-how, materials, notes, records, designs, ideas, inventions, improvements, devices, developments, discoveries, compositions, trade secrets, processes, methods and/or techniques, whether or not patentable or copyrightable, that are conceived, reduced to practice or made by Consultant alone or jointly with others in the course of performing the Services or through the use of Confidential Information (collectively, 111nventions"). Consultant agrees to sign, execute and acknowledge or cause to be signed, executed and acknowledged without cost, but at the expense of Company, any and all documents and to perform such acts as my be necessary, useful or convenient for the purposes of perfecting the foregoing assignments and obtaining, enforcing and defending intellectual property rights in any and all countries with respect to Inventions. It is understood and agreed that Company or Company's designee shall have the sole right, but not the obligation, to prepare, file, prosecute and maintain patent applications and patents worldwide with respect to Inventions. Upon the termination of this Agreement, or upon Company's earlier requests, Consultant will deliver to Company all property relating to, and all tangible embodiments of, Inventions in Consultant's possession or control. Consultant agrees that if, in the course of carrying out the Consulting, Consultant incorporates into any Invention developed hereunder any invention, improvement, development concept, discovery or other proprietary subject matter owned by Consultant or in which Consultant has an interest ("Item"), Consultant will inform Company in writing thereof, and Company is hereby granted and shall have a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license to make, have made, modify, reproduce, display, use and sell such Item as part of or in connection with the exploitation of such Invention. Consultant agrees that if Company is unable because of Consultant's unavailability, mental or physical incapacity, or for any other reason, to secure Consultant's signature to apply for or to pursue any application or registration for any intellectual property rights covering any Invention, then Consultant hereby irrevocably designates and appoints Company and its duly authorized officers and agents as Consultant's agent and attorney-in-fact, to act for and in Consultant's behalf
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'performing the Services,' which is a defined term and relates to the work outlined in Exhibit A. The changed text replaces this with 'carrying out the Consulting,' which is undefined. The contradicted text relies on 'performing the Services' to define ownership of inventions. By using 'carrying out the Consulting' in the changed text, it creates ambiguity about whether inventions made during 'Consulting' but not explicitly 'Services' are still subject to the same ownership provisions, leading to a direct conflict and inconsistency in the contract's terms and obligations. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.
